title: lomatium nuttallii var. alpinum
intro: Lomatium nuttallii var. alpinum, also known as Alkali Biscuitroot, is a perennial plant native to the western United States, characterized by its small yellow flowers and edible roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like eczema. The health benefits of Lomatium nuttallii var. alpinum include its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for various forms of consumption and topical application.
